title: glechoma nepetella
intro: Glechoma nepetella, also known as Russian melissa or creeping Melissa, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb in the mint family native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Glechoma nepetella are believed to provide various health benefits, including relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. In herbal preparations, Glechoma nepetella is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
